concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Ways to store graph data, such as adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Algorithms for visiting all nodes in a graph, like Depth-First Search or Breadth-First Search.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction, meaning connections are bidirectional.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path in a graph that starts and ends at the same vertex, visiting other vertices along the way.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes that have already been explored during a graph traversal.

concepts.name: Parent Tracking
concepts.short_definition: Keeping track of the node from which the current node was visited during traversal.

concepts.name: DFS Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Using DFS to identify cycles by checking for back edges to already visited nodes that are not the immediate parent.

concepts.name: Back Edge
concepts.short_definition: An edge in a DFS traversal that connects a node to an ancestor in the DFS tree (excluding the immediate parent).

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Union (DSU)
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that tracks a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint subsets, useful for cycle detection.

concepts.name: DSU Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Detecting cycles by iterating through edges and checking if the two endpoints of an edge already belong to the same set.
